changelog:
Eight-phase pass: streaming-timeout fix, smarter pool, realtime dashboard, per-API-key isolation, research-driven region/endpoint routing, and forwards-compatible Claude family-version derivation.

Phase 1 - correctness fixes:
- Streaming timeout: replaced the 5-min Client.Timeout wall-clock cap that was killing long thinking-mode generations with a per-stream idleTimeoutReader plus ResponseHeaderTimeout for the handshake. The body cancels only when no bytes arrive within the idle window.
- Stats canonicalization: model ids are lowercase + dot-to-dash + thinking-suffix-stripped before being recorded so claude-opus-4-7 / claude-opus-4.7 / claude-opus-4.7-thinking collapse to one analytics row. One-shot SQLite migration in stats.Init merges legacy duplicate rows.
- Filter default migration: configs predating the FilterClaudeCode/FilterEnvNoise/FilterStripBoundaries fields silently loaded them as false. Added FilterDefaultsApplied marker so the on-by-default migration runs at most once per install; explicit operator off-toggles after migration are preserved.
- Disabled accounts in totals: apiGetStatus exposes activeQuotaTotal/Used/activeTokens/activeRequests for enabled accounts only; dashboard prefers the active aggregate so disabling an account immediately stops its credits from counting.
- Honor Retry-After down to 1s: dropped the 5s clamp floor on upstream-supplied Retry-After. retryAfterAbsoluteMin=1s prevents a 0/negative header from pinning the account.
- 402 OVERAGE -> 1h cooldown: monthly-quota exhaustion does not recover on a per-minute timescale. New pool.RecordQuotaExhaustion parks the account for ~1h until the periodic refresh notices the reset.
- API key copy button: GET /admin/api/apikeys/{id}/reveal under admin auth, plus a per-row copy button.
- Relabeled the OpenAI thinking-format DeepSeek option as a format hint, not a model selector.

Phase 2 - pool intelligence:
- Configurable AWS region via KIRO_API_REGION env or admin UI.
- Three pool strategies selectable per install: smooth weighted round-robin (default), least-used, random.
- Region shape validation rejects malformed entries; the env and update paths now warn-log dropped values.

Phase 3 - realtime dashboard:
- Replaced the 3-second poll with a WebSocket push at /admin/ws/status. recordSuccess, recordFailure, and the lazy account-info refresh each broadcast a JSON snapshot to subscribed admin browsers.
- Auth via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol admin-password.<password> subprotocol (browsers can't set custom headers on WS upgrades). Constant-time compare, echoed back to complete the handshake.
- Polling fallback at 10s armed even when WS is healthy. Hub uses 16-buffer per-subscriber channel; slow consumers dropped via non-blocking select.
- README documents the reverse-proxy upgrade-header passthrough and the access-log password-leak warning.

Phase 5 - research-driven endpoint rewrite:
- codewhisperer.<region>.amazonaws.com only resolves in us-east-1 (NXDOMAIN elsewhere, per jwadow/kiro-gateway #58). Outside us-east-1 the proxy now substitutes runtime.<region>.kiro.dev.
- kiroRESTBaseForRegion follows the same split.
- Multi-region failover (KIRO_API_REGIONS env, comma-separated) expands the endpoint chain across listed regions. maxEndpointChain=12 caps worst-case attempts. No surveyed competitor proxy does this; opt-in.

Phase 5/6/7 - per-API-key isolation:
- New per-key Group restricts which Kiro accounts the key can route to (combined with Account.Groups membership). Empty = no restriction.
- Per-key Models allowlist enforced at /v1/models AND at request time. Empty = all allowed (default).
- Admin UI: checkbox grid for the allowlist from new GET /admin/api/available-models, with Select-all / Clear / refresh controls and an extras textbox for ids not yet in the catalog. Dotted/dashed pairs collapse; canonical pre-tick mirrors the request-time alias matcher so dotted stored ids correctly tick against dashed catalog rows (avoided a security regression where a round-trip edit would silently strip the allowlist).

Phase 8 - forwards-compatible Claude family derivation:
- Removed four hardcoded family-version lists (pool.modelRouteAliases, config.apiKeyModelAliases, proxy.kiroModelToAnthropicID switch, plus 13 mechanical rows in proxy.modelMapOrdered) and replaced them with a single derivation rule: any claude-<opus|sonnet|haiku>-<digits>{.,-}<digits> id resolves to the dotted upstream form via pure string transformation. New minor versions like claude-opus-4-8 (or 4-10, 5-0, 10-2) work without a code change as long as Anthropic stays in the family + 1-2 digit major + 1-2 digit minor shape.
- Reordered ParseModelAndThinking so the family derivation runs BEFORE the explicit modelMapOrdered table walk; otherwise the loose strings.Contains match on the bare claude-sonnet-4 row would swallow claude-sonnet-4-X derivations.
- Pinned both forwards-compat (future-minor + double-digit) AND legacy paths (claude-3-*, gpt-*, dated sonnet-4-20250514, bare claude-sonnet-4) with explicit table tests so a future cleanup can't silently regress either.
- The dated suffix (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) is excluded from the derivation by the 1-2 digit minor cap and falls through to the existing explicit row.

Tests: ~25 new top-level Test functions plus ~40 sub-table cases across config, pool, proxy, stats. All packages green on go test ./...; -race recommended in a CGO-enabled CI lane.

Deferred: prompt-cache passthrough. Field research showed only kirocc (18 stars) attempts upstream cachePoint injection, only for tool-level cache_control; no third-party verification. Implementing speculatively against an unverified upstream contract is not worth the risk.
